report_name: GlassWorm Campaign Evolves, Uses Zig-Based Dropper to Infect All Developer IDEs

threat_actors.name: GlassWorm
threat_actors.description: Operators of a persistent cyber-espionage campaign targeting software developers. The group was observed using a Zig-based dropper distributed via a malicious Open VSX extension to infect developer IDEs.
threat_actors.goals: Achieve deep, persistent access to developer workflows for software supply chain compromise, source code theft, and credential harvesting.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Initial access was gained by tricking developers into installing a malicious Open VSX extension named 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker', a form of software dependency compromise.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The dropper scans the file system and registry to find the installation paths of all other Integrated Development Environments (IDEs) on the compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Office Application Startup
attack_patterns.description: The malware infects the configuration files or plugins of all installed IDEs to ensure it is executed every time the developer starts their work environment, achieving persistence in a method conceptually identical to Office Application Startup.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: By compromising the developer's IDE, the attackers can inject malicious code into legitimate software projects during the build process, representing the primary impact of the attack.

malware.name: Zig-based Dropper
malware.description: A dropper written in the Zig programming language, distributed via a malicious Open VSX extension named 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker'. It scans for and infects all installed IDEs on a developer's machine to establish persistence.

campaigns.name: GlassWorm
campaigns.description: An ongoing cyber-espionage campaign targeting software developers. A recent evolution involves using a Zig-based dropper delivered via a malicious VSX extension to infect developer IDEs and compromise the software supply chain.
campaigns.objective: Compromise developer workstations to enable software supply chain attacks.

identities.name: Software Developers
identities.description: The primary target group of the GlassWorm campaign, compromised via malicious IDE extensions.

identities.name: WakaTime
identities.description: A legitimate productivity tool whose branding and name were impersonated by the malicious Open VSX extension 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker' to deceive developers.

identities.name: Open VSX
identities.description: A marketplace for VS Code extensions that was used to distribute the malicious 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker' extension.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Open VSX Extension Hosting
infrastructure.description: The Open VSX marketplace was used to host and distribute the malicious extension 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker' containing the Zig-based dropper.

indicators.name: GlassWorm Malicious VSX Extension
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: GlassWorm Malicious VSX Extension Filename
id: 7f5b1a9e-8c4d-4f8a-b9e1-3d2c7b6a9f01
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of a file with the name of the malicious VSX extension used in the GlassWorm campaign.
references:
  - https://www.wiu.edu/cbt/cybersecurity_center/cybersecurity_news.php
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-11
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but a legitimate extension could be created with a similar name.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the malicious Open VSX extension file 'specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker' associated with the GlassWorm campaign.

indicators.name: Zig Build Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Zig Build Command Execution
id: 1e8c4d9a-3b2a-4c1d-b8e9-f0a1b2c3d4e5
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'zig build', which may indicate the presence of the Zig programming language toolchain. While often legitimate for developers using Zig, it was noted as a feature of the GlassWorm dropper and is uncommon in many environments.
references:
  - https://www.wiu.edu/cbt/cybersecurity_center/cybersecurity_news.php
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-11
tags:
  - attack.t1059
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains: 'zig build'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use by developers working with the Zig programming language.
level: low
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the 'zig build' command, which could indicate the presence of the Zig toolchain used by the GlassWorm dropper. This is a low-confidence indicator as it may be legitimate developer activity.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
